items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

Brenda Fricker, who became the first female Irish Oscar winner for acting with My Left Foot, has died ag

items.hook: Ginevra is a feminine given name.
items.body: It was occasionally used in medieval and Renaissance Italy in reference to Queen Guinevere, King Arthur’s queen in the popular Arthurian legends. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Argura also called Argyra (Ἀργυρᾶ) was a town of ancient Euboea near Chalkis, but its exact location is unknown.
items.body: Modern scholars differ as to its location, with the current village of Lefkandi in the estuary of the Lilas River being identified by Denis Knoepfler Tritle places his remains on the hill of Vrachos in Vasiliko. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Trollied is a British television sitcom about employees in a fictional supermarket named "Valco", which debuted on Sky One on 4 August 2011 and ended on 23 December 2018.
items.body: The series was filmed in a purpose-built replica supermarket in the Bottle Yard Studios in Bristol throughout April and May 2011 for the first series with the second series being filmed in June and July 2012. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: The Boy Downstairs is a 2017 American romantic comedy-drama film written and directed by Sophie Brooks, in her feature directorial debut.
items.body: The film stars Zosia Mamet, Matthew Shear, Deirdre O'Connell, Sarah Ramos and Diana Irvine. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: "Heartache All Over the World" is a song by British musician Elton John and lyricist Bernie Taupin from John's twentieth studio album Leather Jackets (1986).
items.body: It was released as the album's lead single in September 1986, charting at number 45 in the UK singles chart and at number 55 on the US Billboard Hot 100, and reaching the top 10 in Australia, where it peaked at number seven. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit
